summary: Replaced the 680-line assembly implementation of Dolphin AI with a matched C implementation based on prime decomp, added a public AI header, and moved shared OS time/tick clock macros into OSTime.h so AI, CARD, and DVD can share them instead of duplicating local definitions.

key_files.role: Deleted old hand/disassembled AI assembly source.
New matched C implementation for Dolphin AI routines and interrupt handlers.
New AI API header with callback typedefs, register declaration, and public function prototypes.
Centralized __OSBusClock and OS time-to-tick conversion macros used by AI and other subsystems.
Removed duplicated OS bus/timer clock macros now provided by OSTime.h.
Removed duplicated OS bus/timer clock macros now provided by OSTime.h.
Switched AI object from asm/dolphin/ai/ai.s.o to src/dolphin/ai/ai.c.o.

what_changed: Added src/dolphin/ai/ai.c with C versions of AIRegisterDMACallback, AIInitDMA, AIStartDMA, stream play-state/sample-rate/volume accessors, AIInit, AIS/AID interrupt handlers, callback stack switching, and __AI_SRC_INIT.
Removed asm/dolphin/ai/ai.s, which previously contained the AI implementation from 0x803503E4 through 0x80350C88.

Added include/dolphin/ai/ai.h with AISCallback and AIDCallback typedefs, an absolute-address volatile AI register array declaration `extern vu32 __AIRegs[8] : 0xCC006C00;`, and public AI function declarations.
Added OS bus/timer clock declarations and conversion macros to include/dolphin/os/OSTime.h, including OSSecondsToTicks, OSMillisecondsToTicks, and OSNanosecondsToTicks.
Removed duplicate __OSBusClock and tick macro definitions from CARDBios.c and dvd.c.
Updated obj_files.mk so the build links `src/dolphin/ai/ai.c.o` instead of `asm/dolphin/ai/ai.s.o`.
PR body states the implementation was based on prime decomp.
smart_moves: Moved common timer conversion macros into OSTime.h rather than keeping per-file definitions in CARD and DVD; this made AI's OSNanosecondsToTicks usage reusable and reduced local duplication.

Modeled hardware registers as volatile absolute-address arrays (`__AIRegs` in the AI header, and existing `__DSPRegs` via dolphin/dsp/dsp.h), which keeps MMIO access explicit and close to the original register-manipulating assembly.
Preserved interrupt safety around callback registration, DMA initialization, sample-rate switching, and SRC initialization with OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts.
Preserved hardware state during stream/DSP sample-rate changes by saving play state, volumes, and sample-rate bits, muting during reconfiguration, then restoring state afterward.
Used a small static asm function for `__AICallbackStackSwitch`, keeping the difficult stack-swap sequence exact instead of forcing an all-C rewrite where matching would be fragile.
Kept unused locals and state variables in `__AI_SRC_INIT` where needed for matching evidence, rather than over-cleaning the decompiled control flow.
decomp_lessons: For low-level Dolphin libraries, first promote repeated hardware clock/tick macros into the canonical OS header; later matched modules can then include the shared declarations instead of redefining them.
When matching MMIO-heavy code, volatile absolute-address extern arrays can make bit operations compile close to original load/store sequences while still producing readable C.
Callback and interrupt code often needs exact OS context transitions; AI handlers use OSClearContext and OSSetCurrentContext around callback invocation, mirroring the runtime behavior from assembly.

Hardware reconfiguration routines may intentionally mute audio and preserve/restore register state around SRC initialization; do not simplify these sequences unless the resulting assembly and behavior are verified.
A mixed C plus static asm approach is acceptable when a tiny stack or ABI-sensitive fragment blocks a clean C match.
External decomp sources such as prime decomp can be useful references, but the PR evidence only states it was based on prime decomp and does not document detailed adaptation choices.
naming_conventions: `AISCallback` and `AIDCallback` are used as callback typedef names for stream and DMA interrupts.
Public AI APIs use the Dolphin-style `AI...` prefix, for example `AIInitDMA`, `AISetStreamPlayState`, `AIGetStreamSampleRate`, and `AIInit`.
Internal globals and static helpers preserve SDK-like double-underscore names such as `__AIS_Callback`, `__AID_Callback`, `__CallbackStack`, `__AI_init_flag`, and `__AI_SRC_INIT`.
The static helper for stream sample-rate setting uses lower-case-with-underscores after the prefix: `__AI_set_stream_sample_rate`.
The new AI header guard is `DOLPHIN_AI_AI`, matching the submitted file rather than a conventional `_H` suffix.
Timer constants/globals use descriptive names matching the algorithm: `bound_32KHz`, `bound_48KHz`, `min_wait`, `max_wait`, and `buffer`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted a single assembly file into one C translation unit and changed obj_files.mk from the asm object to the C object, a clear full-function replacement tactic.
Used explicit masks such as `~0x20`, `~1`, `~2`, `~0x40`, `0xFF`, and `0xFF00` against `__AIRegs` to reproduce the original rlwinm/ori/rlwimi-style bit manipulation.

Used `OSNanosecondsToTicks(31524)`, `OSNanosecondsToTicks(42024)`, `OSNanosecondsToTicks(42000)`, `OSNanosecondsToTicks(63000)`, and `OSNanosecondsToTicks(3000)` in `AIInit` to express the magic timing constants seen in assembly.
Preserved busy-wait loops in `__AI_SRC_INIT` that poll `__AIRegs[2]` and OSGetTime, rather than abstracting them away.
Used `register AIDCallback` and static asm with `fralloc`/`frfree`, manual r1 save/restore, and `mtlr cb; blrl` for the callback stack switch.
Added an `#ifndef M2CTX` guard around the absolute-address annotation for `__OSBusClock`, avoiding that syntax in m2ctx contexts while retaining it for normal builds.
review_feedback: No issue comments or line review comments were present in the provided slice, so there is no specific human feedback beyond approvals.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or refine Dolphin AI symbols such as __AI_SRC_INIT, __AIS_Callback, or __AID_Callback.
Search for subsequent changes adding missing AI header prototypes for AIResetStreamSampleCount or AISetStreamTrigger, which are implemented in ai.c but not shown in the new ai.h.
Check later OSTime.h changes for type corrections to __OSBusClock, since this PR declares it as u32 while removed local definitions used vu32.
Look for later PRs that replace static asm callback stack-switch code with cleaner C or shared stack-switch helpers.
Search other Dolphin library matches that reuse OSNanosecondsToTicks and the OS_BUS_CLOCK/OS_TIMER_CLOCK macros introduced here.
